context.goal: Given one or more Instagram usernames (or profile URLs), use the Apify Instagram Scraper to fetch full profile data and the latest 12 posts, then store everything in MongoDB so the FakeShield detection pipeline can consume it.

tech_stack.scraper: Apify REST API – actor shu8hvrXbJbY3Eb9W (Instagram Scraper)

var.name: APIFY_TOKEN
var.description: set via the APIFY_TOKEN environment variable

var.name: MONGODB_URI
var.description: set via the MONGODB_URI environment variable

field.name: about.country
field.description: Country the account is registered in

field.name: ownerId
field.description: Profile ID that owns this post

collection.name: instagram_profiles
collection.document_structure: { "_id" : ObjectId(), // auto "instagram_id" : "528817151", // from id field "username" : "nasa", "full_name" : "NASA", "biography" : "...", "followers_count" : 96323377, "follows_count" : 80, "posts_count" : 4519, "igtv_video_count" : 171, "highlight_reel_count" : 7, "is_business_account" : true, "business_category" : "Government Agencies", "is_verified" : true, "is_private" : false, "joined_recently" : false, "profile_pic_url" : "https://...", "external_url" : "https://www.nasa.gov/", "profile_url" : "https://www.instagram.com/nasa", "about": { "date_joined" : "August 2013", "date_joined_as_timestamp" : 1375315200, "country" : "United States", "former_usernames" : 0, "date_verified" : null }, "related_profiles" : [ { "username": "iss", ... } ], "scraped_at" : ISODate("2026-04-09T..."), // datetime.utcnow() "scan_id" : "uuid-string" // link back to scan }

collection.name: instagram_posts
collection.document_structure: { "_id" : ObjectId(), "post_id" : "3697661021377407101", "short_code" : "DNQuxufAih9", "owner_username" : "nasa", "owner_id" : "528817151", "type" : "Sidecar", "caption" : "Under the sea...", "hashtags" : ["NASA", "Hubble"], "mentions" : ["NASAHubble"], "post_url" : "https://www.instagram.com/p/DNQuxufAih9/", "display_url" : "https://...", "likes_count" : 601022, "comments_count" : 1882, "video_view_count": null, "timestamp" : ISODate("2025-08-12T16:19:28Z"), "is_pinned" : true, "scraped_at" : ISODate("2026-04-09T..."), "scan_id" : "uuid-string" }

collection.name: scan_history
collection.document_structure: { "_id" : ObjectId(), "scan_id" : "uuid-string", "input" : "@nasa", "platform" : "instagram", "status" : "completed", // pending | running | completed | failed "profiles_found": 1, "posts_found" : 12, "created_at" : ISODate("2026-04-09T..."), "completed_at": ISODate("2026-04-09T...") }

step.title: Create the scraper module
step.instructions:
Replace the existing placeholder with a real implementation.
CLASS: InstagramScraper
METHOD: scrape_profile(self, usernames: list[str], scan_id: str) -> list[dict] 1. Build the Apify actor run request: - POST to https://api.apify.com/v2/acts/shu8hvrXbJbY3Eb9W/runs - Header: Authorization: Bearer {APIFY_TOKEN} - JSON body: { "directUrls": [ "https://www.instagram.com/{username}/" for each username ], "resultsType": "details", "resultsLimit": 1, "addParentData": false } 2. Poll the run status endpoint every 5 s until status is SUCCEEDED or FAILED (GET https://api.apify.com/v2/acts/shu8hvrXbJbY3Eb9W/runs/{runId}) 3. Once SUCCEEDED, fetch dataset items: GET https://api.apify.com/v2/datasets/{defaultDatasetId}/items?format=json 4. Return the raw list of profile dicts from the dataset. 5. Raise a RuntimeError if the run FAILS or times out after 300 s.
All HTTP calls must use the requests library with a 30 s timeout. Log each polling attempt at DEBUG level using Python's logging module.

step.title: Create the database persistence module
step.instructions:
Implement three functions using pymongo.
FUNCTION: get_db() - Connect to MONGODB_URI from os.environ - Return the database object for DB_NAME ("fakeshield") - Cache the MongoClient in a module-level variable (singleton pattern)
FUNCTION: save_profile(profile_data: dict, scan_id: str) -> str - Map the raw Apify profile dict to the instagram_profiles schema defined in the MongoDB schema section above (snake_case keys). - Add "scraped_at": datetime.utcnow() and "scan_id": scan_id. - Upsert into the instagram_profiles collection using filter={"username": profile_data["username"]}. - Return the upserted or matched document's _id as a string.
FUNCTION: save_posts(profile_data: dict, scan_id: str) -> int - Iterate over profile_data.get("latestPosts", []) - Map each post to the instagram_posts schema. - Parse "timestamp" string to a Python datetime object. - Bulk-upsert using pymongo's bulk_write with UpdateOne operations, filter={"post_id": post["id"]}, upsert=True. - Return the count of posts upserted/updated.
FUNCTION: create_scan_record(scan_id: str, input_value: str, platform: str) -> None - Insert a new document into scan_history with status="pending".
FUNCTION: update_scan_record(scan_id: str, status: str, profiles_found: int, posts_found: int) -> None - Update the matching scan_history document with final status, counts, and completed_at=datetime.utcnow().

step.title: Wire the route to the real scraper + DB
step.instructions:
Update the POST /api/detect-impersonation handler:
1. Extract "profile_url" (username or URL) and "platforms" from the request body. 2. Generate a UUID scan_id. 3. Call create_scan_record(scan_id, profile_url, "instagram"). 4. Instantiate InstagramScraper and call scrape_profile([cleaned_username], scan_id). - Strip leading "@" and extract username from full URLs if needed. 5. For each profile returned: a. Call save_profile(profile, scan_id) b. Call save_posts(profile, scan_id) 6. Call update_scan_record with status="completed" and counts. 7. Pass the saved profile dicts to the existing FakeAccountDetector. 8. Return the detection result as JSON (keep the existing response structure).
Handle errors: - If scraping fails, call update_scan_record with status="failed" and return a 500 JSON error response. - Wrap the entire handler in a try/except block.

step.title: Update requirements.txt
step.instructions: Ensure the following packages are listed (add if missing): requests>=2.31.0 pymongo[srv]>=4.6.0 python-dotenv>=1.0.0 sentence-transformers>=2.7.0 fuzzywuzzy>=0.18.0 python-Levenshtein>=0.25.0 instaloader>=4.10.3 flask>=3.0.0 flask-cors>=4.0.0

step.title: Fix the typo in bio_similarity.py
step.instructions: Rename the method "calculase_similarity" → "calculate_similarity" in both the method definition and every call site within the file. This is a known bug listed in the README's Known Gaps section.

step.title: Create .env.example
step.instructions:
Create a sample env file with all required variables:
MONGODB_URI=mongodb+srv://:@cluster0.h5dhu2u.mongodb.net/?appName=Cluster0 DB_NAME=fakeshield APIFY_TOKEN=your_apify_api_token_here API_HOST=0.0.0.0 API_PORT=8001 FLASK_DEBUG=True
NOTE: Set API_PORT=8001 to match the frontend's expected port (fixes the port mismatch documented in the README's Known Gaps section).

rules.rule: Never hard-code secrets (API keys, MongoDB URIs, passwords) in source files. Always read from os.environ or python-dotenv.
All MongoDB operations must use upsert=True to be idempotent – re-scanning the same username must not create duplicate documents.
Map Apify camelCase field names to Python snake_case when building MongoDB documents.
Always handle the case where Apify fields may be None/missing – use .get() with sensible defaults.
Parse "timestamp" strings from Apify posts to Python datetime objects before storing. Use dateutil.parser.isoparse() or datetime.fromisoformat().
Log scraper progress at INFO level and polling at DEBUG level using Python's built-in logging module.
Do not import or run InstagramScraper at module load time; instantiate it only inside route handlers.
Keep the existing FakeAccountDetector interface intact – the scraper only feeds it data, it does not replace it.
Add docstrings to every new public function and class.
The entire scraping + DB pipeline must complete within 60 s for a single username in normal conditions. Use async polling with sleep intervals, not a blocking delay.

copilot_prompt.code_example:
import os, time, requests
ACTOR_ID = "shu8hvrXbJbY3Eb9W" APIFY_TOKEN = os.environ["APIFY_TOKEN"] BASE = "https://api.apify.com/v2"
def run_and_fetch(usernames: list[str]) -> list[dict]: headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {APIFY_TOKEN}"}
# 1. Start actor run run_resp = requests.post( f"{BASE}/acts/{ACTOR_ID}/runs", json={ "directUrls": [f"https://www.instagram.com/{u.lstrip('@')}/" for u in usernames], "resultsType": "details", "resultsLimit": 1, "addParentData": False, }, headers=headers, timeout=30, ) run_resp.raise_for_status() run_data = run_resp.json()["data"] run_id = run_data["id"] dataset_id = run_data["defaultDatasetId"]
# 2. Poll until finished deadline = time.time() + 300 while time.time() < deadline: status_resp = requests.get( f"{BASE}/actor-runs/{run_id}", headers=headers, timeout=30 ) status_resp.raise_for_status() status = status_resp.json()["data"]["status"] if status == "SUCCEEDED": break if status in ("FAILED", "ABORTED", "TIMED-OUT"): raise RuntimeError(f"Apify run {run_id} ended with status: {status}") time.sleep(5) else: raise RuntimeError(f"Apify run {run_id} timed out after 300 s")
# 3. Fetch results items_resp = requests.get( f"{BASE}/datasets/{dataset_id}/items", params={"format": "json"}, headers=headers, timeout=30, ) items_resp.raise_for_status() return items_resp.json()

from datetime import datetime from pymongo import MongoClient, UpdateOne import os
_client = None
def get_db(): global _client if _client is None: _client = MongoClient(os.environ["MONGODB_URI"]) return _client[os.environ.get("DB_NAME", "fakeshield")]
def save_profile(raw: dict, scan_id: str) -> str: db = get_db() doc = { "instagram_id" : raw.get("id"), "username" : raw.get("username"), "full_name" : raw.get("fullName"), "biography" : raw.get("biography"), "followers_count" : raw.get("followersCount"), "follows_count" : raw.get("followsCount"), "posts_count" : raw.get("postsCount"), "igtv_video_count" : raw.get("igtvVideoCount"), "highlight_reel_count": raw.get("highlightReelCount"), "is_business_account" : raw.get("isBusinessAccount", False), "business_category" : raw.get("businessCategoryName"), "is_verified" : raw.get("verified", False), "is_private" : raw.get("private", False), "joined_recently" : raw.get("joinedRecently", False), "profile_pic_url" : raw.get("profilePicUrl"), "external_url" : raw.get("externalUrl"), "profile_url" : raw.get("url"), "about" : raw.get("about"), "related_profiles" : raw.get("relatedProfiles", []), "scraped_at" : datetime.utcnow(), "scan_id" : scan_id, } result = db.instagram_profiles.update_one( {"username": doc["username"]}, {"$set": doc}, upsert=True, ) return str(result.upserted_id or doc["username"])
